entries.rootCause: Import path is incorrect — wrong number of ../ levels or file moved to a different directory.

fix.description: Count ../ levels from the importing file to the target file and correct the relative path.
fix.patch: Verify file exists at the resolved path; adjust ../ depth to match actual directory structure.

signature.messagePattern: Property '(.+)' does not exist on type '(.+)'

entries.rootCause: Accessing a property or method not defined on the class/interface. Often caused by typo in property name or missing declaration in base class.

fix.description: Check the class definition for the correct property name. Add the property if missing, or fix the typo.
fix.patch: Open the type/class definition, verify available properties, and correct the access or add the missing declaration.

entries.rootCause: Object accessed before initialization. Common in Phaser create() where an object is used before being constructed.

fix.description: Reorder creation statements so that the object is initialized before it is accessed.
fix.patch: Move the construction/assignment statement above the first usage in create() or the relevant lifecycle method.

entries.rootCause: Texture key used in code does not match the key registered in asset-pack.json. Often a spelling difference or missing frame number suffix.

fix.description: Compare the texture key in code with asset-pack.json and use the exact key from the asset registry.
fix.patch: Open asset-pack.json, find the correct key, and replace the mismatched key in the source file.

entries.rootCause: Animation key referenced in code does not exist in animations.json. Key may have been renamed or never created.

fix.description: Cross-reference with animations.json to find the correct key, then update the code.
fix.patch: Open animations.json, verify the animation key exists, and correct the reference in the source file.

entries.rootCause: Scene class not registered in main.ts Phaser config. Scene.start() targets a key that was never added via game.scene.add().

fix.description: Import the scene class in main.ts and add it to the Phaser game config's scene list.
fix.patch: Add `import { XScene } from './scenes/XScene';` and `game.scene.add('XScene', XScene);` in main.ts.

entries.rootCause: Infinite recursion, typically from a method calling itself without a proper base case, or circular scene transitions.

fix.description: Add a base case to recursive calls, or break the circular dependency in scene/method calls.
fix.patch: Trace the call stack to identify the recursive loop and add a termination condition.

signature.messagePattern: Asset key '(.+)' used in code but not found in asset-pack.json

entries.rootCause: Code references texture/audio keys that do not exist in the asset registry. Caught by scanning code for string literals and cross-referencing with asset-pack.json.

fix.description: Either add the missing key to asset-pack.json or correct the key in source code.
fix.patch: Grep all string literals used with this.add.image/sprite/audio, compare against asset-pack.json keys.

signature.messagePattern: Config field '(.+)' accessed in code but not defined in gameConfig.json

entries.rootCause: Code accesses a gameConfig field that was never defined. Often caused by GDD→config copy errors or renamed fields.

fix.description: Add the missing field to gameConfig.json with the correct value wrapper format.
fix.patch: Grep code for gameConfig field accesses, compare with gameConfig.json top-level keys.

signature.messagePattern: Scene key '(.+)' used in scene\.start\(\) but not registered in main\.ts

entries.rootCause: scene.start() or scene.launch() targets a scene key that is not registered in main.ts. Will fail at runtime with 'Scene not found'.

fix.description: Register the missing scene in main.ts and ensure LevelManager.LEVEL_ORDER includes it if it's a game level.
fix.patch: Grep all scene.start/launch calls, compare target keys against main.ts scene registrations.

signature.messagePattern: Animation key '(.+)' referenced in code but not defined in animations.json

entries.rootCause: Code plays an animation by key that doesn't exist in animations.json. Three-layer check: asset-pack.json → animations.json → code animKeys.

fix.description: Verify the three-layer chain: asset frames exist → animation defined → code key matches.
fix.patch: Check asset-pack.json has frames, animations.json has the animation entry, and code uses the exact key.

entries.rootCause: TypeScript interfaces/types must be imported with the `type` keyword under verbatimModuleSyntax. Missing `type` causes build errors.

fix.description: Add `type` keyword to interface/type imports: `import { type X }` instead of `import { X }`.
fix.patch: Scan imports, identify which imported names are interfaces/types, and add the `type` keyword.

signature.messagePattern: Override method '(.+)' narrows visibility from (.+) to (.+)

entries.rootCause: TypeScript does not allow narrowing method visibility in overrides (e.g., public→protected). This is a common mistake when copying template code.

fix.description: Match or widen the visibility of the overriding method to match the base class.
fix.patch: Check base class method visibility and ensure the override uses the same or wider access modifier.

signature.messagePattern: LEVEL_ORDER\[0\] is '(.+)' but first game scene is '(.+)'

entries.rootCause: LevelManager.LEVEL_ORDER[0] still has the default template value instead of the actual first game scene key.

fix.description: Update LEVEL_ORDER[0] in LevelManager.ts to match the actual first game scene key.
fix.patch: Set LEVEL_ORDER to the scene keys defined in main.ts scene registrations.
